summary: The initial urinary albumin/creatinine ratio (ACR) above 0.3 correlates strongly with diagnosed preeclampsia and predicts worse maternal and neonatal outcomes such as preterm delivery, intrauterine growth restriction, and low birth weight due to underlying placental dysfunction. This makes the first ACR an important marker for identifying high-risk pregnancies at baseline.
